topic: I worked the graveyard shift at a 24-hour gas station in the middle of nowhere
There is a recording on my answering machine from someone I have never met
The new house came with a baby monitor in the attic that we did not install
There is a door in my basement that wasn't there last week
My brother went missing for six hours when we were kids and came back wrong
My night shift at the call center kept getting calls from a number that didn't exist
I went hiking alone and found a campsite that had been waiting for me
I found a cassette tape labeled DO NOT PLAY in my late uncle's attic
We moved into a house that was suspiciously cheap and now I know why
I have a daughter who is not in any of the family photos but she is here
I deliver food at night and I have learned which addresses to never go to twice
My smart doorbell keeps recording someone standing in our hallway
There is a video on my drone's SD card that I never recorded
I was a security guard at an empty hospital that was scheduled for demolition
I remembered a babysitter we had when I was seven that no one else in my family remembers
My GPS keeps telling me to take an exit that does not exist
I found my grandfather's old camcorder and the last recording is from after his death
My grandmother's house has a room that everyone in the family pretends doesn't exist
I have dreamed of the same house my entire life. I found it on Google Street View last week and it is real.
There was an empty house at the end of my street that the adults all whispered about
My imaginary friend from when I was four has started visiting my own daughter
My dashcam keeps saving footage of roads I have never driven
I was the only employee on the night shift at a small motel off Route 6
The man my mother is dating is wearing my father's wedding ring
My new neighbor knows things about me he should not know
My phone's location history shows me visiting an abandoned house every night at 3AM. I have never been there.
The Polaroid camera I inherited only takes pictures of one specific room
My router logs show my dead father's laptop connecting to my network every night
I took a wrong turn on a back road and ended up in the same town twice
My sleep tracker has forty-three nights of missing data over two years. I have no memory of those nights.
My smart home devices started responding to a voice that is not mine. The voice knows my full name.
My smart lock app shows someone entering my house every night while I sleep
I drove through a town that was not on any map I owned
My truck broke down on a highway with no other cars for three hours, then one came
I have a twin I did not know about until last summer
My birth certificate was signed by a doctor who died two years before I was born
My electricity bill is for a house that was demolished in nineteen eighty seven
Night shift at a self storage facility on Old Lapeer Road where unit two seventeen had a tenant nobody had ever seen
The previous tenant of my apartment left a calendar marked he comes by every Tuesday at nine
I have hiked the same three mile trail every October for nine years and this year there was a fourth mile marker
I bought a box of unmarked VHS tapes at an estate sale for two dollars
I bought a used phone online and someone is still receiving messages on it
My elderly neighbor's voice assistant kept asking her questions she did not understand
I worked at a remote weather station and something started leaving footprints in the snow
My daughter's kindergarten drawings all show a tall man in a hallway we have never lived in
The same grey sedan has been at my grocery store every visit for six months
There is a room in my house that only appears in the mirror
My dog barks at the exact same spot in the kitchen at the exact same time every night
My doorbell camera records a child knocking on my door every night at three seventeen but no one is ever there when I check
My house key fits a stranger's front door three blocks away from where I live
I keep finding receipts in my jacket pockets for places I have never been
My security camera records 47 seconds of footage I never saw happen every night at 2:13 AM
My smoke detector only beeps when no one is home
My phone call log shows a three minute call every Tuesday at three fourteen AM to a number I do not recognize
I found a roll of undeveloped film in my dead grandmother's drawer and the photos were taken last month
Every wedding photo on our wall shows a fifth person standing behind us
My mailbox keeps getting letters addressed to people who never lived at my address
My wife does not appear in any of our wedding photos. No one else finds this strange.
My ceiling has a water stain in the shape of my own face that appeared the day I moved in
My calendar has a recurring Friday meeting I did not create at an address that no longer exists
My cat brings me small objects every morning from a basement that was bricked over in nineteen seventy four
Our family Christmas card from nineteen ninety eight has gained an extra child every December since
My dentist found a tooth I do not remember growing and the root goes deeper than any of my others
My dishwasher only finishes its cycle when I am asleep and stops if I stay up to watch
My doctor's office has been calling to remind me about appointments I do not remember scheduling
My front door keeps getting locked from the inside while I am at work
My downstairs neighbor sweeps his floor every night at two thirty AM and his apartment has been empty for nine months
My building's elevator stops at floor thirteen every Thursday at seven PM but there is no thirteenth floor
The backseat seatbelt of my car is buckled every morning and I drive alone
The word HELLO appears in the steam on my bathroom mirror every morning and I shower alone
My ceiling fan rotates counterclockwise every Sunday and the chain does not turn with the blades
The closet light in my apartment is on every morning and the fixture has no bulb in it
My building's fire escape ends three feet above the ground with a clean metal cut and it used to reach the ground
My fitness tracker logs a heart rate spike at exactly eleven forty seven PM every night while I am asleep
The Amazon delivery driver for my route has been the same man for eleven years and he has not aged
My Ring doorbell logs motion every night at three seventeen AM and the recording shows an empty porch
My garden has a row of footprints leading from my back porch to my fence with no return prints
My garage door opens every Tuesday at six fourteen AM and there is no automatic opener
The tools on my garage workbench rearrange themselves into letters every Wednesday night
The snack aisle at my grocery store has been moving one section closer to the entrance every week
There is a wear pattern in my hallway carpet that leads to a wall with no door
My HOA newsletter has been listing my house with a different family's name for six issues straight
The houses on my street go from forty seven to forty nine and nobody remembers a forty eight ever existing
Faces in my kindergarten class photo have been getting crossed out one at a time in the same marker
Every laundromat I use returns clothes that are not mine and they are always children's clothes
My library card has been checking out books for two years in a city I have never visited
My microwave clock shows eighty eight colon eighty eight every night at exactly two AM
My bathroom mirror reflects a wall clock behind me but there is no clock on that wall
The neighbor's child waves at my upstairs window every morning but my upstairs is an attic with no windows
My neighbors are in Greece but their kitchen light turns on every night at two forty seven AM
The newspaper on my porch arrives every morning with tomorrow's date printed on it
My car's odometer drops by twelve miles every Sunday morning
I throw out the same expired can of soup every week and it keeps coming back to my pantry
My passport photo has been visibly aging while I have not
There is a contact in my phone I did not add and the profile photo is the outside of my own front door
There are wet footprints leading away from my backyard pool but the pool has been drained for six years
My power bill has been charging me for a second meter I do not own
There is a passenger in my rearview mirror at every red light but the backseat is empty when I turn around
My grocery receipt listed items I never bought at prices from twenty years ago
Every recipe in my grandmother's recipe box has been altered since I started using them
My sister sends me the same photograph every Sunday but the timestamps are from thirty years apart
A smoke detector in my studio apartment chirps from a hallway my floor plan does not include
The spare key under my flowerpot is replaced with a different key every Monday morning
My Spotify plays a podcast every night at three AM that does not exist on Spotify
My building's stairwell loses a floor between four and five every Friday at midnight
The back left burner on my stove is warm when I come home and the gas has been off at the wall valve
The street signs on my block have been changing letter by letter for three months
The streetlight outside my window has been broken six months but my room is still lit from outside every night
My thermostat resets itself to sixty eight every night at exactly three fourteen AM
My backyard is bigger every time I mow it and the back fence keeps moving away
Every book on my shelf is dog-eared on the same page but I never read them
There is a water stain on my bedroom ceiling shaped like a person that moves closer to my bed each night
There is a child's car seat buckled in my back seat every morning but I have no children
The door to my childhood bedroom is a wall now but I still hear the knob turn
My bedroom closet is deeper on the inside than the wall it sits in allows
The chairs at my dining table are pulled out a little more every day as if someone keeps sitting down
There is a washed dish in my sink every morning but I eat out every night
My doorbell camera records me leaving the house every night at three but I never leave
Every morning my car driver's seat is adjusted for someone much taller than me
Everything in my refrigerator has the same expiration date a few weeks in the future
Every morning there is an extra pair of shoes by my front door that fits me perfectly
My staircase has fourteen steps but some nights there is a fifteenth
There is a grocery list on my fridge in my handwriting for food I am allergic to that I never wrote
There are photos on my phone taken inside my house of me sleeping that I never took
There is a clean rectangle on my garage floor where a second car has been parked but I own one car
My gas tank is always full no matter how far I drive
The hallway to my bedroom gets longer at night
The knives in my kitchen rearrange themselves by size every night
My mailbox keeps getting letters addressed to me from me postmarked next year
There are muddy work clothes in my hamper every Monday but I work from home
A car has been parked across the street every night for a month and it is my car
There is a light switch in my house that controls nothing and last week it started getting warm
The peephole in my apartment door shows a hallway that is not the one outside
All my houseplants lean toward one empty corner of the room away from the windows
My reflection in the bathroom mirror blinks a second after I do
My bathroom scale shows two weights when I step on it but I weigh myself alone
There is a head shaped dent in the pillow next to mine every morning but I sleep alone
A wet handprint appears on the inside of my shower glass while I shower but I shower alone
I wake up tucked into bed so tightly I cannot move and I live alone
My old basement television turns on by itself every night and plays a channel that does not exist
When my television is off the black screen reflects a room that is not mine
There is a voicemail on my phone in my own voice that I never recorded
My hallway wallpaper has been peeling in a pattern that spells a word and the word is half complete
A man has been appearing in my parents wedding photos over the last year moving closer to my father each scan
My welcome mat moves four feet to the left every Tuesday night and now it is hanging off the porch
My wife has started reading aloud in her sleep from a book that does not exist
The wind chime in my backyard rings only on still nights and the pattern is Morse code for my middle name
There is a window in my apartment that is not on the floor plan
I bought an old house at auction and the previous owner had nailed every window shut
My alarm is set for three seventeen every night, and I never set it.
A hatch appears overnight in a hallway ceiling, opening onto an attic far larger than the house below.
Small handprints appear on the inside of my back windows every morning.
My back seat is soaking wet every morning and smells of lake water.
A hole in my backyard is deeper every morning, with no dirt piled beside it.
A basement floods with warm clean water every night and is completely dry again by dawn.
My hung towel is damp and warm every morning, though I only shower at night.
My bathroom door locks itself from the inside while the room is empty.
A bathtub fills itself with warm water every night.
The birds on my lawn all face my window, motionless, arranged in neat rows.
My blanket is pulled to the floor every night, though I sleep without moving.
A mug with my name appears in the office break room each morning, still warm.
A fireplace bricked over years ago grows warm at night with the sound of a fire crackling behind it.
The same passenger boards my bus every day and steps off one pace behind me.
A wall calendar is missing the same date every month and those days cannot be remembered.
Every face in my phone's camera roll is being scratched out, one photo per day.
My car stalls every time I try to drive past one particular house on my street.
Footsteps cross my ceiling every night, though I live on the top floor.
A grandfather clock chimes thirteen at midnight and on the thirteenth something moves.
Every clock in the house runs backward for one hour before dawn.
Clothes in my exact size that I never bought keep appearing in my closet.
My closet door is open one inch wider every single morning.
A fresh pot of coffee is made before I wake, with two cups already poured.
The crawlspace under a house is furnished like a lived-in living room the owner never set up.
Every curtain on my street closes at the same exact second at dusk.
My dashcam catches the same hitchhiker on the same empty road every night.
My dog refuses to enter one corner of the yard where the grass never grows.
The doorway to a kitchen grows slightly shorter every morning until the resident must crawl through it.
The hair I pull from my shower drain is a color I have never had.
Fresh tire tracks lead into my driveway each morning and stop at the garage, with no car.
My wireless earbuds play soft whispering when nothing is connected to them.
A button appeared in my office elevator for a floor the building does not have.
The down escalator at the mall runs far longer than the floor below allows.
A front porch grows one extra step every month until the staircase descends below street level.
My bathroom faucet drips in a pattern that always spells the same word.
There is a fax machine in my basement I never installed, printing notes in my own handwriting.
My back gate is open every morning, latched from the outside.
My phone flashlight casts a second shadow of me that moves on its own.
A For Sale sign appears in my yard at night, listing me as the seller with a sold date.
A three-bedroom house keeps revealing a fourth bedroom in a different location each time.
My phone's front camera turns on by itself at night and records the ceiling above my bed.
A garden gnome I never bought moves around my yard, always facing the house.
My glasses grow a little stronger every morning, blurring whatever is near.
My car's GPS lists Home as an address two hours away I have never lived at.
In the gym mirror, my reflection keeps lifting after I have stopped.
My handwriting changes a little every day into someone else's.
Empty hangers in a closet swing as if coats were just lifted away.
Three knocks come from inside my headboard every night.
My coat pockets fill with dark grave dirt and a different key every morning.
The same jogger passes my window every morning, a little slower and grayer each day.
A kitchen junk drawer fills with one new unfamiliar key every morning.
My house keys are warm on their hook every morning, as if just used.
My suitcase in the closet is packed for a trip I never planned, and it repacks itself.
The medicine cabinet behind my mirror rearranges itself nightly, and new pills appear.
Words appear in the fog on my mirror after every shower, in a child's handwriting.
A full glass of water appears on my nightstand every night.
There is a desk in my office with my nameplate that I never sat at, and it is always warm.
My old disconnected phone, dead in a drawer, rings every night at the same minute.
A power outlet on an interior wall blows cold air, revealing a hidden room behind it.
A staircase descending into the ground appears in a pantry of a house that has no basement.
There is a parking level the elevator stops at but the stairs never reach.
My office parking spot has my name and a death date painted on the curb.
A pen on my desk writes one line each night, finishing a letter I never began.
The photos in my frames are swapped each night for the same people, a year older.
I look years older in every photo taken of me within the same week.
My porch light burns out every night and is replaced with a warmer bulb by morning.
My printer spits out a page each night: a photo taken inside my own house.
My car radio only finds a station reading numbers; last night it read my license plate.
Gas receipts appear in my car from a station that closed twenty years ago.
My reflection in shop windows lags a step behind me as I walk.
The last stall in the office restroom is always occupied, the same shoes for months.
A ring of coarse salt appears around the bed each morning.
A scarecrow appeared in the field behind my house, closer to my door every week.
A second toothbrush, damp and used, appears in my holder.
